title: centaurea boissieri var. humilis
intro: Centaurea boissieri var. humilis, also known as the low mountain knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Centaurea boissieri var. humilis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its flowers, leaves, and roots are typically used for medicinal purposes.
